message:
fix(repl): accept PSYNC2 CONTINUE <replid> suffix in parse_psync_reply

Redis 7 primaries emit either `CONTINUE` (PSYNC1 backward-compat) or
`CONTINUE <replid>` (PSYNC2) on a successful partial-sync. Our parser
only accepted the bare `CONTINUE` form and rejected anything with a
suffix — so replicas talking PSYNC2 to a Redis 7 primary saw the
handshake bomb out with `PsyncReplyStateMismatch`. Fix the parser
and add replay coverage.

crates/fr-repl/src/lib.rs
  * `parse_psync_reply` now handles three shapes:
      - "CONTINUE"              → Ok(PsyncReply::Continue)
      - "CONTINUE <replid>"     → Ok(PsyncReply::Continue)   (PSYNC2)
      - "CONTINUE <replid> extra_token…"
                               → Err(PsyncReplyStateMismatch)
    Implementation: after the leading `kind` token, consume the next
    token and require the iterator to be exhausted before returning
    Ok; anything past the optional replid is a shape violation.
  * Removes the dead pre-check branch that short-circuited on
    `line == "CONTINUE"` before the tokenizer — unreachable now
    that the tokenizer path handles it identically.
  * Unit-test additions under `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`:
      - parse_psync_reply_accepts_continue_and_fullresync:
        `CONTINUE <replid>` parses as Continue.
      - parse_psync_reply_rejects_invalid_shape:
        `CONTINUE replid extra`, `FULLRESYNC replid 42 extra`
        both Err.

crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs
  * New `#[test] replication_continue_apply_accepts_psync2_replid_suffix`:
    drives a full primary→replica handshake, then feeds a
    `CONTINUE 1111111111111111111111111111111111111111` reply plus
    a backlog slice through
    `apply_replication_sync_payload(…)` and asserts the replica
    applies downstream SETs (`beta = 2`) without handshake error.
    This is the end-to-end property the parser change enables.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
